metadata.title:
FUEL FAMINE.

Local Dealers Ask Large Sums in


Advance for Wood.

pages.raw_text:
A fuel famine has struck Spokane
and many blocks, private dwellings,
and even hospitals are running on
short rations.


Local wood dealers have in many
cases demanded and received ad-
vance payment of two-thirds of the
value of the wood, often amounting
managers of the business blocks.


The cause of the present dearth
of fuel is largely due to difficulty
in securing cars for transportation,
the greater part of the supply of
most of the dealers being out of the
city.


1t is an absolute fact that wood
has been hauled into the city on
wagons from points % miles dis
tant.


A local hospital, which had con-
tracted for many cords, was mott
fed last week that unforeseen diff
tiCulties prevented the fulfillment
of the contract by the dealer and
for several days it's wood has been
supplied only through the kindness
of another dealer.


Most of the wood handled by To-
cal wood dealers is received from
Loon Lake, Deer Park, Standpoint
and other points equally as distant,
it being shipped in over the Spo-
kane Falls & Northern railway and
the Northern Pacific, and it is al
most impossible to obtain wood on
short notice.


The consumption of wood in Spo-
kane is estimated at the present
time to be considerably over 125
cords per day.
